etymology.description: semantic 廾(廾) (gǒng, "two hands") + phonetic 已(已) (yǐ) -> different
etymology.interpretation: Two hands 廾(廾) (gǒng, "two hands") suggest the act of giving or handling around, implying variations or multiple possibilities. The phonetic component 已(已) (yǐ) provides the sound 'yì'. Together, they are used to convey the meaning of 'different', as multiple hands can produce different outcomes.
etymology.simplification: 异 is a simplified form in which the components are altered, using 已(已) (yǐ) without the implication of multiple hands 廾(廾).

examples.chinese: 这个城市的文化异于其他地方(這個城市的文化異於其他地方)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège chéngshì de wénhuà yì yú qítā dìfāng.
examples.english: The culture of this city is different from other places.

examples.chinese: 他的想法总是很异新(他的想法總是很異新)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de xiǎngfǎ zǒng shì hěn yì xīn.
examples.english: His ideas are always very novel.

examples.chinese: 我们对问题的看法异同明显(我們對問題的看法異同明顯)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen duì wèntí de kànfǎ yìtóng míngxiǎn.
examples.english: Our differing views on the issue are obvious.
